perturbation.original_text: 3.13 Eutectix Warranty. Eutectix warrants that the Product (i) will conform to the Specifications, (ii) will be free from manufacturing defects (including all defects in workmanship and any defects in materials that were not specified by Liquidmetal or Customer), ) will be free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, charges, claims, or adverse interests of any kind, (iii) will comply with, in all stages of manufacture and distribution to Liquidmetal or Customer, the terms of Section 10. The foregoing representations and warranties shall survive delivery, inspection and payment and shall run in favor of Liquidmetal and its Affiliates, and their respective successors, assigns and customers, both direct and indirect.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.13 Eutectix Warranty. Eutectix warrants that the Product will substantially conform to the Specifications to the best of its ability, but does not guarantee perfection. In the event of minor deviations, Eutectix will work with Liquidmetal to find mutually acceptable solutions. The foregoing representations and warranties shall survive delivery, inspection and payment and shall run in favor of Liquidmetal and its Affiliates, and their respective successors, assigns and customers, both direct and indirect.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the warranty from 'will conform to the Specifications' to 'will substantially conform to the Specifications to the best of its ability', Eutectix reduces the strictness of the warranty. This contradicts Section 3.9 which states 'Acceptance of the Liquidmetal Product shall be based on characteristics that are measurable by a quality system and designed to demonstrate compliance with the Specifications.'. The original text implies strict compliance, while the changed text allows for some deviation. The in-text contradiction emerges regarding how strictly the products must adhere to the specifications for acceptance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.9 Acceptance. Acceptance of the Liquidmetal Product shall be based on characteristics that are measurable by a quality system and designed to demonstrate compliance with the Specifications. Unless Liquidmetal notifies Eutectix that the Liquidmetal Product does not meet the Specifications within thirty (30) calendar days after receipt of the Liquidmetal Product, then the Liquidmetal Product shall be deemed Accepted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the warranty from 'will conform to the Specifications' to 'will substantially conform to the Specifications to the best of its ability'. This introduces ambiguity regarding the required level of compliance. The contradicted text, Section 3.9, states that acceptance is based on characteristics 'designed to demonstrate compliance with the Specifications'. The original language in 3.13 implied strict compliance, which aligned with 3.9. The changed language allows for deviations, creating a direct conflict with the strict compliance requirement in 3.9. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists regarding the standard for acceptance.

perturbation.original_text: 2.5 Bailment of Equipment. Eutectix will provide facility space for the Licensed Equipment in a Eutectix-owned or leased secure and protected property that is restricted from unauthorized access or viewing (the "Eutectix Property"). Eutectix shall maintain the Licensed Equipment only in such designated Eutectix Property and shall not relocate the Licensed Equipment from such Eutectix Property without the prior written consent of Liquidmetal. Once Eutectix receives the Licensed Equipment, Eutectix will acknowledge in writing the receipt thereof. Eutectix's acknowledgement of receipt of the Licensed Equipment will constitute an acknowledgement that Eutectix has received and accepted and possesses the Licensed Equipment on bailment for the benefit of Liquidmetal and that such receipt of the Licensed Equipment is not subject to the terms and conditions of any applicable laws pertaining to sales and/or secured transactions. The Licensed Equipment: (a) is and shall remain the sole property of Liquidmetal, (b) shall be made available for reasonable inspection upon at least three (3) weeks prior written request by Liquidmetal, such inspection not to occur more than once per year during the Term, to be conducted with minimal business disruption to Eutectix and to be conducted at Liquidmetal's sole cost and expense and Eutectix shall, at its own expense: (c) keep the Licensed Equipment in a suitable place, safe from loss or damage; (d) subscribe to an insurance policy from an insurance company reasonably acceptable to Eutectix covering the Licensed Equipment at full replacement value against fire, theft and such other normal business risks, with a waiver of subrogation in favor of Liquidmetal and with Liquidmetal to be named as an additional insured and loss payee, and provide, upon receipt of a written request from Liquidmetal, a certificate evidencing such insurance, and comply with all requirements associated with such insurance policy; (e) keep the Licensed Equipment in good working order and condition, excepting normal wear and tear, and perform all regular and routine maintenance and repairs on the Equipment; (f) perform no act or omission inconsistent with Liquidmetal's sole ownership of the Licensed Equipment, nor attempt to sell, assign, loan, donate, mortgage, pledge or in any other manner permit the Licensed Equipment to be encumbered; (g) comply with all reasonable directions given by Liquidmetal regarding (A) the inspection of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement and in connection with any non-ordinary or non-routine revisions or alterations to the Licensed Equipment, (B) the removal and shipment of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, and (C) protecting or perfecting Liquidmetal's interest in the Licensed Equipment, including by executing and permitting Liquidmetal to file financing statements and other documents with respect thereto, at Liquidmetal's expense; and (h) shall be liable for all loss or damage to Licensed Equipment, except for normal wear and tear of the Licensed Equipment.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 Bailment of Equipment. Eutectix will provide facility space for the Licensed Equipment in a Eutectix-owned or leased property (the "Eutectix Property"). While Eutectix will make reasonable efforts to restrict unauthorized access or viewing, complete security cannot be guaranteed. Eutectix shall maintain the Licensed Equipment only in such designated Eutectix Property and shall not relocate the Licensed Equipment from such Eutectix Property without the prior written consent of Liquidmetal. Once Eutectix receives the Licensed Equipment, Eutectix will acknowledge in writing the receipt thereof. Eutectix's acknowledgement of receipt of the Licensed Equipment will constitute an acknowledgement that Eutectix has received and accepted and possesses the Licensed Equipment on bailment for the benefit of Liquidmetal and that such receipt of the Licensed Equipment is not subject to the terms and conditions of any applicable laws pertaining to sales and/or secured transactions. The Licensed Equipment: (a) is and shall remain the sole property of Liquidmetal, (b) shall be made available for reasonable inspection upon at least three (3) weeks prior written request by Liquidmetal, such inspection not to occur more than once per year during the Term, to be conducted with minimal business disruption to Eutectix and to be conducted at Liquidmetal's sole cost and expense and Eutectix shall, at its own expense: (c) keep the Licensed Equipment in a suitable place, safe from loss or damage to the extent reasonably possible; (d) subscribe to an insurance policy from an insurance company reasonably acceptable to Eutectix covering the Licensed Equipment at full replacement value against fire and theft, and provide, upon receipt of a written request from Liquidmetal, a certificate evidencing such insurance, and comply with all requirements associated with such insurance policy; (e) keep the Licensed Equipment in reasonably good working order and condition, taking into account normal wear and tear, and perform routine maintenance and repairs on the Equipment; (f) perform no act or omission intentionally inconsistent with Liquidmetal's sole ownership of the Licensed Equipment, nor attempt to sell, assign, loan, donate, mortgage, pledge or in any other manner permit the Licensed Equipment to be intentionally encumbered; (g) comply with all reasonable directions given by Liquidmetal regarding (A) the inspection of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement and in connection with any non-ordinary or non-routine revisions or alterations to the Licensed Equipment, (B) the removal and shipment of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, and (C) protecting or perfecting Liquidmetal's interest in the Licensed Equipment, including by executing and permitting Liquidmetal to file financing statements and other documents with respect thereto, at Liquidmetal's expense; and (h) shall be liable for all loss or damage to Licensed Equipment, except for normal wear and tear of the Licensed Equipment, to the extent that such damage results from Eutectix's negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.explanation: The modifications to Section 2.5 weaken the strict bailment conditions initially established. By changing 'secure and protected property that is restricted from unauthorized access or viewing' to a standard where 'complete security cannot be guaranteed' and adding qualifiers like 'to the extent reasonably possible,' 'taking into account normal wear and tear,' and limiting liability to damages resulting from 'Eutectix's negligence or willful misconduct', a conflicting interpretation of the bailment agreement is created. The original text established strict liability and security, while the modified version introduces reasonable efforts and intent as the benchmark. Specifically this perturbation contradicts with 2.5(f): perform no act or omission inconsistent with Liquidmetal's sole ownership of the Licensed Equipment, nor attempt to sell, assign, loan, donate, mortgage, pledge or in any other manner permit the Licensed Equipment to be encumbered. The contradiction arises as the phrase 'intentionally inconsistent' in the modified text, changes the meaning and the level of required guarantee of such restriction. Thus, there's an ambiguity if Eutectix is going to make a action, or in-action that is not intentional but goes against Liquidmetal's ownership of the equipment. It would be up for interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5 Bailment of Equipment. Eutectix will provide facility space for the Licensed Equipment in a Eutectix-owned or leased property (the "Eutectix Property"). While Eutectix will make reasonable efforts to restrict unauthorized access or viewing, complete security cannot be guaranteed. Eutectix shall maintain the Licensed Equipment only in such designated Eutectix Property and shall not relocate the Licensed Equipment from such Eutectix Property without the prior written consent of Liquidmetal. Once Eutectix receives the Licensed Equipment, Eutectix will acknowledge in writing the receipt thereof. Eutectix's acknowledgement of receipt of the Licensed Equipment will constitute an acknowledgement that Eutectix has received and accepted and possesses the Licensed Equipment on bailment for the benefit of Liquidmetal and that such receipt of the Licensed Equipment is not subject to the terms and conditions of any applicable laws pertaining to sales and/or secured transactions. The Licensed Equipment: (a) is and shall remain the sole property of Liquidmetal, (b) shall be made available for reasonable inspection upon at least three (3) weeks prior written request by Liquidmetal, such inspection not to occur more than once per year during the Term, to be conducted with minimal business disruption to Eutectix and to be conducted at Liquidmetal's sole cost and expense and Eutectix shall, at its own expense: (c) keep the Licensed Equipment in a suitable place, safe from loss or damage to the extent reasonably possible; (d) subscribe to an insurance policy from an insurance company reasonably acceptable to Eutectix covering the Licensed Equipment at full replacement value against fire and theft, and provide, upon receipt of a written request from Liquidmetal, a certificate evidencing such insurance, and comply with all requirements associated with such insurance policy; (e) keep the Licensed Equipment in reasonably good working order and condition, taking into account normal wear and tear, and perform routine maintenance and repairs on the Equipment; (f) perform no act or omission intentionally inconsistent with Liquidmetal's sole ownership of the Licensed Equipment, nor attempt to sell, assign, loan, donate, mortgage, pledge or in any other manner permit the Licensed Equipment to be intentionally encumbered; (g) comply with all reasonable directions given by Liquidmetal regarding (A) the inspection of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement and in connection with any non-ordinary or non-routine revisions or alterations to the Licensed Equipment, (B) the removal and shipment of the Licensed Equipment, including upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, and (C) protecting or perfecting Liquidmetal's interest in the Licensed Equipment, including by executing and permitting Liquidmetal to file financing statements and other documents with respect thereto, at Liquidmetal's expense; and (h) shall be liable for all loss or damage to Licensed Equipment, except for normal wear and tear of the Licensed Equipment, to the extent that such damage results from Eutectix's negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 2.5 stated that Eutectix will provide 'secure and protected property that is restricted from unauthorized access or viewing'. This was modified to 'While Eutectix will make reasonable efforts to restrict unauthorized access or viewing, complete security cannot be guaranteed'. The original text established strict liability and security, while the modified version introduces reasonable efforts and intent as the benchmark. Also the modification 'intentionally inconsistent' creates an ambiguity as any action, or in-action that is not intentional but goes against Liquidmetal's ownership of the equipment would be up for interpretation. Thus the modification introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty.

perturbation.original_text: 14.3 Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either Party without the written consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; provided, however, that Liquidmetal shall have the right, without the prior written consent of Eutectix, to assign its warranty rights and other rights hereunder with respect to specific Liquidmetal Products to the Customers of such Liquidmetal Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.3 Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either Party without the written consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, unless the assignment is to a direct competitor, in which case consent may be withheld at the sole discretion of the party. Furthermore, Liquidmetal shall have the right, without the prior written consent of Eutectix, to assign its warranty rights and other rights hereunder with respect to specific Liquidmetal Products to the Customers of such Liquidmetal Products.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition 'unless the assignment is to a direct competitor, in which case consent may be withheld at the sole discretion of the party' introduces an ambiguity regarding the standard for withholding consent. The original text states consent 'shall not be unreasonably withheld,' implying an objective standard, but the modification allows for subjective withholding in the case of a competitor. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding what it means for the consent to be reasonably withheld or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.3 Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either Party without the written consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, unless the assignment is to a direct competitor, in which case consent may be withheld at the sole discretion of the party. Furthermore, Liquidmetal shall have the right, without the prior written consent of Eutectix, to assign its warranty rights and other rights hereunder with respect to specific Liquidmetal Products to the Customers of such Liquidmetal Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that consent for assignment 'shall not be unreasonably withheld.' The changed text introduces an exception for assignments to direct competitors, stating that consent 'may be withheld at the sole discretion of the party.' This creates a contradiction because the 'unreasonably withheld' standard implies an objective assessment, while 'sole discretion' implies a subjective one. The contradiction arises because both clauses are included in the same paragraph and apply to the same situation, creating a direct conflict in the standard applied to withholding consent. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, which clearly indicates that the change introduces the contradiction.
